text:
Nora Roberts, writing J.D.
Robb, puts futuristic lieutenant Dallas supernatural showdown with most seductive criminal: vampire.
ancient coin whisks American woman modern-day earl into past-and into each other's arms-in stirring tale from Mary Blayney.
When city girl visits Scottish castle Ruth Ryan Langan's story, thrust into timeless romance with mighty Highland laird.
Mary McComas gives unhappy wife magic-carpet ride into alternate reality show grass isn't always greener.
Robb (aka Nora Roberts) indeed headliner, this all-new four-novella anthology definitely doesn't suffer from standout single syndrome this one's killer, filler.
Robb's opener, courageous female with troubled past clashes with bloodthirsty, unnaturally powerful mystery promises enthralled victims immortality: charismatic artist, much worse?
Blayney follows with story enigmatic coin that transports American tourist oddly aristocratic bartender into Regency-era adventure.
Langan's, another unsuspecting American time-traveler stumbles into romance with 15th-century Scottish warlord believes she's kidnapped wife.
McComas's, bored housewife takes magic carpet ride alternate universe do-over marriage.
Though they don't always keep straight face, occasionally tipping from fantasy into farce instance, canny medieval Scottish ruler blithely accepting 21st-century designer pant suit regulation female barbarian dress such lapses minor; biggest fault readers will find that these intriguing characters taken away quickly (especially Blayney's frustratingly rushed resolution).
